title: Morning Light
author: Original Work
lyrics:
The morning light comes streaming in,
Through windows high and wide,
I rise to greet another day,
With hope I cannot hide.

The birds are singing on the wing,
Their melodies so free,
I'll join their chorus, clear and strong,
And find my own key.

Each new day brings chances bright,
For happiness and grace,
I'll seize the moments as they come,
And run my daily race.

title: River's Journey
author: Original Work
lyrics:
The river flows from mountain high,
To valleys far below,
It carries on its gentle song,
Wherever it may go.

The fish swim deep in waters cool,
The reeds bow low and sway,
The river knows no boundaries,
It finds its own way.

Like water flowing free and wild,
Our spirits too can roam,
From troubles dark to brighter shores,
We always find our home.

title: Stars Above
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Stars above shine bright tonight,
Like diamonds in the sky,
I think of you beneath their light,
And wonder how time flies.

Your eyes reflect the cosmos deep,
Your voice like zephyr breeze,
When I'm with you I feel complete,
At perfect ease.

The stars will fade at break of dawn,
But love remains anew,
Forever shining just like them,
In skies of midnight blue.

title: The Wanderer's Return
author: Original Work
lyrics:
I've traveled roads both long and worn,
Through storms and sunny days,
Now homeward bound my heart does turn,
To familiar ways.

The inn I left so long ago,
The garden by the door,
My family waits with open arms,
As they have before.

No gold nor glory called me back,
Just love I hold so true,
The wanderer's return at last,
To lives I've always knew.

title: Autumn Leaves
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Autumn leaves in golden hue,
Are falling all around,
The summer's warmth has passed away,
With winter's approach found.

The trees stand bare against the sky,
So dignified and tall,
They wait for spring to bring again,
New growth upon them all.

Like leaves that fall and turn to earth,
We too shall return,
To nourish new beginnings strong,
And lessons we have learned.

title: Dancing in the Rain
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Let the rain come down on me,
I'll dance beneath the sky,
Each drop that falls washes clean,
The troubles from my eye.

The puddles bounce beneath my feet,
The thunder rolls afar,
I'm dancing free and wild and loud,
Like a shooting star.

The storm will pass, the sun will shine,
And I will still be here,
Dancing through the rain and shine,
With nothing held in fear.

title: Mountain High
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Stand tall against the mountains high,
Where eagles make their nest,
Your spirit soars above the clouds,
With courage manifest.

No valley low can hold you back,
No ocean stop your way,
The mountain peak awaits your climb,
Beginning today.

From summit high the view's so grand,
All fears seem far behind,
You touched the sky and found your wings,
And left your chains unwind.

title: Firelight Stories
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Gather round the firelight,
Where shadows dance and play,
We share our tales of wonder,
Of yesterday and today.

The embers glow like rubies red,
The sparks ascend on high,
Each story told brings warmth within,
As hours pass us by.

The fire dies but memories,
Burn bright within our heart,
Of firelight and stories told,
That never really part.

title: City Lights
author: Original Work
lyrics:
City lights like stars below,
Illuminate the night,
I walk these streets you're not on now,
And hold your memory tight.

The taxi cabs pass yellow bright,
Like schools of fish at sea,
You're somewhere near yet far away,
From this urban spree.

The city that never sleeps,
Now lullaby does sing,
Of loneliness without your hand,
And the peace your voice would bring.

title: Sea of Dreams
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Sail away on sea of dreams,
Where imagination roams,
To islands far in distant realms,
That live within our homes.

The tides pull strong on hearts of men,
To harbors yet unknown,
The compass spins to point the way,
Through waters not yet shown.

Our voyage long on dreaming sea,
Brings treasures rare to find,
The real treasures we discover,
Are treasures of the mind.

title: Midnight Train
author: Original Work
lyrics:
On the midnight train I ride,
Through darkness all alone,
The rhythm of the railway ties,
My weary heart to home.

The lights of towns flash past the windows,
Like shooting stars above,
Each station passed brings me closer,
To the one I love.

The journey's long but soon I'll see,
The platform where you wait,
And hold you close,再也不分离,
At last we're not too late.

title: Heart's Reflection
author: Original Work
lyrics:
In the mirror I see your face,
Where once was only mine,
Now every feature that I trace,
Holds love within its line.

The reflection shows a brighter me,
Since you became a part,
Of all the dreams I'm dreaming now,
And all that's in my heart.

You're the reflection of my soul,
In glass so crystal clear,
The truest love I have ever known,
Is standing somewhere near.

title: Garden's Song
author: Original Work
lyrics:
In my garden sweet and small,
The flowers bloom and grow,
The roses red, the lilies white,
A rainbow's gentle glow.

The bees do buzz from bloom to bloom,
The butterflies dance free,
The garden sings its song of life,
For all the world to see.

And as I tend my garden plot,
With love I sow and reap,
I find that peace grows deeper still,
When roots are buried deep.

title: Moonlit Path
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Walk with me beneath the moon,
On silver lit retreat,
Where shadows dance in moonlit trance,
And shadows softly meet.

The nightingale does serenade,
From branches dark and high,
While we two walk the moonlit path,
Beneath the starry sky.

The moon does guide our wandering feet,
To wonders yet unseen,
On paths of light we walk tonight,
In love's eternal dream.

title: Old photographs
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Old photographs in faded box,
Tell stories we forgot,
Of holidays and birthday parties,
That time has since ungrokked.

The faces young now lined with years,
The smiles now turned to tears,
But love that shined through every frame,
Endures beyond our fears.

I'll frame these memories today,
And hang them on my wall,
A testament to love gone by,
That helped me stand up tall.

title: First Born
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Little one with eyes so bright,
You bring us such delight,
Our family is complete,
With hands and feet so neat.

You laugh and coo and always smile,
Make every trial worthwhile,
Our hearts burst with pride,
Our new one by our side.

We'll guide you every day,
Through work and through your play,
To be the best you can be,
Set free.

title: Homestead
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Four walls that hold our love so true,
Make any house a home,
With you beside me every day,
No place I'd rather roam.

The fireplace crackles warm and bright,
The porch swing sways with ease,
Our homestead built on solid ground,
With every season's breeze.

This land is ours to call our own,
Where two hearts beat as one,
A sanctuary from the world,
Until our days are done.

title: Simple Days
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Simple days are sweetest days,
With coffee and the news,
No grand adventures far away,
Just potted plants and shoes.

The morning light through curtains thin,
A comfortable chair,
A book half read beside the bed,
A life beyond compare.

Give me simplicity and peace,
With nothing grand or new,
The simple days are treasure troves,
When shared with only you.

title: Sailing Home
author: Original Work
lyrics:
The sails are set, the wind is fair,
For harbor lights ahead,
After months upon the sea,
I'm dreaming of my bed.

The gulls cry out their welcome home,
As shore draws ever near,
The harbor bell rings clear and strong,
To touch my waiting ear.

The anchor drops, the sails come down,
I've sailed so far from home,
But nothing feels as sweet as this,
To never more roam.

title: Final Hour
author: Original Work
lyrics:
When my final hour arrives,
And I must say goodbye,
I pray I'll face it without fear,
Beneath the open sky.

I hope to have no regrets,
No sorrow for the days,
That passed me by without a trace,
Or veered from chosen ways.

But if I fail, dear Lord above,
Please welcome me anew,
A prodigal soul returning home,
Whose love was always true.

title: Wildflower Field
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Wildflowers grow in field and meadow,
Where nobody tends or sows,
They find their way through rock and soil,
Against all common shows.

Purple, yellow, white, and blue,
A painter's palette free,
The wilding bloom cares nothing for,
What humans think to be.

I'd rather be a wildflower bold,
Than garden rose refined,
Growing free where winds do blow,
In body, spirit, mind.

title: Farewell to Summer
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Summer sun begins to fade,
The days grow short and cool,
The vacation memories made,
Live on within our school.

The beach days done, the campfire's out,
The sandcastle's washed away,
But golden tan and laughter loud,
Will stay through winter's gray.

We'll hold the sunshine in our hearts,
Till summer comes again,
When warm winds blow and life restarts,
We'll welcome joy and pain.

title: Morning Coffee
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Morning coffee steams so sweet,
The first sip wakes me up,
The newspaper lies flat and neat,
A morning ritual cup.

The radio plays soft and low,
The birds begin to sing,
The sun creeps in through window glow,
A brand new day to bring.

These simple pleasures make me whole,
Contented, calm, and clear,
A cup of coffee warms my soul,
And guides my day with cheer.

title: Letter From Afar
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Your letter came from overseas,
With stamps and postmarks new,
The words you wrote so long ago,
Still ring so tried and true.

You asked about my health and work,
And how the garden grew,
The children tall, the oldest's smart,
Just like you said they'd do.

Though miles divide us, still your words,
Bridge oceans but by sea,
A lifeline stretched across the world,
From someone dear to me.

title: Grandfather's Clock
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Grandfather's clock stands tall and proud,
Its pendulum swings wide,
Each tick and tock marks time's own road,
Where secret hopes reside.

It watched us grow from children small,
To adults with our own,
It marked our triumphs, failures, all,
Through every season's tone.

The clock may have wound down now,
Its brass has turned to green,
But time stands still in memory,
Where love still lingers clean.

title: Dreams We Carry
author: Original Work
lyrics:
We carry dreams from youth to age,
In pockets deep and wide,
Some dreams have faded with the years,
While others still our guide.

The child who dreamed of flying high,
Now finds a different sky,
The girl who dreamed of dancing far,
Now spins in place nearby.

But dreams don't die, they just evolve,
To fit our present true,
A dream of happiness and love,
Is always born anew.

title: Stormy Weather
lyrics:
Storm clouds gather overhead,
The thunder rolls so near,
I weather every tempest strong,
Through all I hold most dear.

The lightning strikes, the rain descends,
Yet shelter I have found,
For those who love stand by my side,
On this their consecrated ground.

title: Cemetery Gates
lyrics:
We gather at the gates of stone,
Where loved ones rest so deep,
The marble angels watch us weep,
And flowers eternally sleep.

The names are worn by time's own hand,
Yet memory clings on strong,
We come to honor those who've passed,
To keep their love where it belongs.

title: Country Road Home
lyrics:
Down the country road I ride,
My horse beneath the moon,
The crickets chirp their evening song,
A gentle summer tune.

The fences line both sides of road,
Where fireflies dance and play,
I'm heading home to those I love,
At end of every day.

title: Sweet Sixteen
lyrics:
She turned sweet sixteen today,
A lady fair and fine,
The boy next door brought flowers round,
And asked if she were kind.

They danced beneath the paper lanterns,
Twirling round the floor,
Two young hearts learning love's first steps,
As they've done before.

title: Empty Nest
lyrics:
The house feels bare since you've flown,
Where laughter once did ring,
The rooms echo with memories,
Of when you spread your wing.

But pride replaces sorrow deep,
For those who learned to soar,
You found your sky beyond our walls,
And that's what love is for.

title: Harvest Moon
lyrics:
The harvest moon hangs big and bright,
On fields of golden grain,
The farmers work through moonlit nights,
To bring in winter's gain.

The combines hum from dusk to dawn,
The silos fill up tall,
Another year of plenty's come,
To feed both great and small.

title: Old Dog's Day
lyrics:
My old dog lies beside the porch,
His bones grow stiff and weak,
He chases squirrels in his dreams,
Though legs no longer seek.

The puppy days have passed him by,
But love remains so true,
A faithful friend through many years,
Still loyal through and through.

title: Train Whistle Moan
lyrics:
The train whistle moans through the night,
A lonely sound so clear,
It's carrying the wanderers,
To destinations near.

The railroad tracks stretch far and wide,
From town to town they go,
The iron horse that roars so loud,
Brings travelers to and fro.

title: Cobblestone Streets
lyrics:
Old cobblestone streets lead to the square,
Where market vendors call,
The smell of bread and flowers sweet,
Fills both great hall and small.

The fountain's spray catches the light,
As children run and play,
This village square has stood for years,
Where life goes on today.

title: Letter to Tomorrow
lyrics:
Dear tomorrow, I'm writing you,
To ask what's in your plan,
Will sunshine follow rain today,
Or will I finally understand?

I fold this letter carefully,
And seal it with my hope,
The future holds a special place,
Where dreams learn how to grope.

title: Rainy Day Blues
lyrics:
The rain beats down on window pane,
The world has turned to gray,
I've got those rainy day blues,
That never seem to go away.

But coffee steams beside my book,
A blanket soft and warm,
Sometimes the rainy days are best,
To shelter from the storm.

title: New Year's Bells
lyrics:
Ring out the bells for New Year's Eve,
Let joy fill every heart,
We say goodbye to year gone by,
And welcome a new start.

The champagne bubbles sparkle bright,
The ball descends so slow,
A thousand voices cheer as one,
As out the old does go.

title: Butterfly Garden
lyrics:
The butterflies drift through the garden,
On wings of orange bright,
They flutter past the rosebushes,
In their graceful, lazy flight.

The children chase but never catch,
These treasures of the air,
A living rainbow flits about,
With beauty beyond compare.

title: Night Owl's Song
lylysics:
I'm an owl that hoots at midnight,
When the world has gone to bed,
I see the stars from my old oak tree,
And hear the thoughts we've said.

The mice may scurry, worms may squirm,
But I just sit and wait,
For dawn to bring the morning light,
When I shall fly to fate.

title: Sleigh Ride Winter
lyrics:
Jingle bells ring through crisp white snow,
Our sleigh rides swift and light,
The horse hooves pound a steady rhythm,
As we bundle up in fur.

The winter wind it nips our nose,
But warmth comes from within,
When shared with friends and loved ones dear,
The cold cannot begin.

title: Desert Wind
lyrics:
The desert wind whispers through the canyons,
Where ancient spirits roam,
The red rocks stand like guardians,
Protecting sacred home.

The tumbleweed rolls past abandoned towns,
Where ghosts still tell their tales,
Of pioneers who sought their fortune,
On winding desert trails.

title: Lighthouse Keeper
lyrics:
I tend the light that guides the ships,
Through fog and storm and night,
My solitary watch begins,
When darkness brings its blight.

The beam sweeps far across the waves,
Where sailors trust my ray,
To bring them safely to the shore,
At end of ocean's way.

title: Springtime Bloom
lyrics:
The crocuses push through the snow,
A promise of warm days,
The robin builds her nest anew,
In tree branches raised in praise.

The streams run high with snowmelt fresh,
The meadows wake from sleep,
Springtime brings new life again,
And promises to keep.

title: Old Barn Red
lyrics:
The old red barn stands tall and proud,
Its weathervane spins slow,
The hayloft holds a century's worth,
Of harvest long ago.

The paint peels but the bones are strong,
Like memories that last,
A weathered barn on farmland wide,
Holds shadows of the past.

title: Distant Shores
lyrics:
Beyond the waves where horizons meet,
My thoughts drift out to sea,
To distant shores where you might walk,
And wonder still of me.

The salt air carries prayers and dreams,
That travel far and wide,
To shores unknown across the deep,
Where love and hope reside.

title: Moonlight Serenade
lyrics:
Under the moonlight I sing to you,
A melody soft and sweet,
The stars above bear witness true,
To love that feels so complete.

Your silver light illuminates,
The path that we both tread,
A moonlight serenade from me,
To the one I cherish instead.

title: Prairie Sunset
lyrics:
The prairie stretches endless wide,
Beneath a burning sky,
Where cattle graze on grass so green,
And cowboys sit nearby.

The sun descends in orange blaze,
A sight both vast and grand,
The prairie holds its breath awhile,
As stars begin to expand.

title: Childhood Summers
lyrics:
Summer days of endless play,
Bare feet on warm soil brown,
The sprinkler's mist a rainbow mist,
When summer sun comes down.

We'd catch the fireflies at dusk,
In mason jars with holes,
Those childhood summers long ago,
Still warm within our souls.

title: Library of Dreams
lyrics:
Within the library of dreams,
The bookshelves reach so high,
The scent of old and musty pages,
Makes grown-ups pause and sigh.

The stories waiting on the shelves,
Hold magic yet untold,
A library of dreams within,
Where wisdom is the gold.

title: Rainbow's Promise
lyrics:
After the storm the rainbow arcs,
A promise from the sky,
That sun will shine through clouds again,
And warm the earth nearby.

The colors bend from red to violet,
Spanning hill and dale,
A rainbow's promise ever holds,
That hope will still prevail.

title: Fishing at Dawn
lyrics:
The mist rises from the lake so still,
My line cast out with grace,
The bobber floats like a duck's nest,
In this quiet peaceful place.

The heron stands on one leg tall,
Awaiting fish so sly,
While I sit patient in my boat,
As morning hours fly by.

title: Autumn Harvest
lyrics:
The orchards groan with fruit so ripe,
The apples ready to fall,
The cider press is cleaned and oiled,
To catch the season's call.

The farmers pick from dusk till dawn,
The harvest bounty store,
Another year of plenty comes,
To feed the rich and poor.

title: Old Guitar's Song
lyrics:
My old guitar sits in the corner,
Unplayed for years gone by,
Its strings still hold their tuning true,
Beneath the dust that lies.

I strum a chord and feel the vibration,
Of music from my past,
The old guitar plays on for me,
As memories hold fast.

title: Morning Dew
lyrics:
The morning dew sits on the grass,
Like diamonds bright and clear,
It catches light before the sun,
Disperses with our fear.

Each droplet holds a million stars,
Reflecting sky so blue,
Morning dew on flowers fresh,
A day brand new and true.

title: Mountain Stream
lyrics:
The mountain stream jumps over rocks,
Its waters pure and cold,
It carries leaves and acorns small,
As silver fish swim bold.

The banks are lined with moss so green,
Where ferns grow in the shade,
A mountain stream that never dries,
Has carved its own ravine.

title: Old Covered Bridge
lyrics:
The old covered bridge spans the creek,
Its boards creak with each step,
The roof keeps out both rain and snow,
While we cross the deck.

Generations pass beneath its beams,
Of wood so old and worn,
A covered bridge stands still today,
Where horses once were born.

title: First Dance
lyrics:
We stepped onto the ballroom floor,
My heart aflutter light,
Your hand fit mine like perfect puzzle,
On that first dance night.

The orchestra played soft and sweet,
We turned beneath the lights,
Our first dance written in my heart,
Among our fondest memories.

title: Farmhouse Kitchen
lyrics:
The kitchen smells of bread and pie,
With flour upon the floor,
The wood stove warms the winter cold,
As thunderstorms roar.

Grandmother's recipes in the box,
Held secrets we still keep,
A farmhouse kitchen gathering place,
Where love is learned to reap.

title: Starlight Campfire
lyrics:
The campfire crackles bright and warm,
Beneath the starry sky,
The marshmallows we roast on sticks,
Make shadows dance nearby.

The stories shared around the flames,
Will live in memory,
A starlight campfire under skies,
Full of wonder and of glee.

title: Ocean's Breath
lyrics:
The ocean breathes with rhythm strong,
Its waves rise and fall,
The salt air fills my lungs with life,
The sea calls to us all.

The tidepools hide in rocky shores,
Where crabs scuttle and play,
An ocean's breath sustains my soul,
At end of each new day.

title: Windmill Spins
lyrics:
The old windmill spins round and round,
Its blades catch winter's breeze,
It pumps the water from the well,
To satisfy our needs.

The creaking metal sounds so loud,
Against the silent sky,
A windmill stands through time so long,
A sentinel nearby.

title: Penny Farthing Ride
lyrics:
I pedaled hard on penny farthing,
My balance rather poor,
The front wheel large, the back quite small,
I hit the garden floor.

But still I mounted once again,
With stubborn determination,
To master this contraption fine,
A marvel of the nation.

title: Midnight snowfall
lyrics:
A midnight snowfall blankets all,
The world turned white and still,
The only sound is snowflake's soft,
That covers window sill.

The children slept with anticipation,
Of snowman and of sled,
A midnight snowfall frozen still,
Beneath the moon that's shed.

title: Cactus Bloom
lyrics:
The desert cactus blooms at night,
A flower rare and fine,
It draws the moth and moon bat,
To taste its sweetness wine.

The bloom will wilt by morning light,
So brief its beauty shown,
A desert cactus blooms at night,
Yet makes the desert home.

title: Riverside Camp
lyrics:
Our campfire smokes by riverside,
The tent pegs firmly set,
The river flows past our camp,
Where we won't be late yet.

The trout are jumping in the shallows,
The bass lurk in the shade,
A riverside camp peaceful spot,
Where memories were made.

title: Old Porch Swing
lyrics:
The porch swing sways in summer breeze,
Where crickets sing all night,
I sit with you in comfortable silence,
Watching fireflies light.

The neighbor's dog barks down the street,
The ice cream truck rolls by,
An old porch swing on summer nights,
Beneath the starry sky.

title: Dawn's First Light
lyrics:
Dawn's first light breaks over hills,
A new day is begun,
The rooster crows to wake the farm,
To greet the morning sun.

Each sunrise brings a chance anew,
To start refreshed and clean,
Dawn's first light upon the world,
Makes all things seem serene.

title: Blacksmith's Anvil
lyrics:
The blacksmith's hammer rings so clear,
Against the anvil hot,
Sparks fly like fireflies at night,
From the iron they wrought.

The horseshoe cooled in water bucket,
Hisses with each dip,
A blacksmith's trade passed down through years,
Is work that none can skip.

title: Old Blue Jay
lyrics:
The old blue jay sits on the fence,
His feathers blue and white,
He caws at us with attitude,
A bully of the birds.

He steals the seeds we put out,
Chases sparrows away,
An old blue jay with attitude,
Makes吵闹 at our feeders all day.

title: Candlelit Dinner
lyrics:
The candles flicker soft tonight,
The table set for two,
Wine poured in crystal glasses gleams,
As violins play through.

A candlelit dinner for two,
With love within the room,
A perfect evening shared together,
Where hearts entwine in bloom.

title: Forest Trail
lyrics:
The forest trail winds through the pines,
Where ferns grow thick and green,
The sunlight filters through the canopy,
A dappled dancing scene.

The woodpecker drums on old bark,
The deer step light and shy,
A forest trail of wonder lies,
Beneath the open sky.

title: Tractor in the Field
lyrics:
The tractor rumbles through the field,
Dust billows in its wake,
The furrows turned in perfect rows,
The farmers up at stake.

The GPS guides every pass,
Technology meets soil,
A tractor works the land today,
With precision tools.

title: Rainy Porch
lyrics:
The rain taps on the porch roof,
A rhythm soft and slow,
I watch the puddles form below,
As raindrops come and go.

The thunder growls in distance far,
The lightning flashes bright,
A rainy porch is peaceful spot,
To spend a stormy night.

title: Snowman Melody
lyrics:
Frosty the snowman was a jolly happy soul,
With a corncob pipe and a button nose,
And eyes made out of coal.

He's a fairy tale they say,
Was made of snow,
But friends we always stay,
In our hearts he'll always glow.

title: Wind Chime Notes
lyrics:
The wind chimes ring when breezes blow,
A melody so clear,
It carries songs from somewhere far,
To ears that want to hear.

Each chime a note of music plays,
Of nature's gentle art,
A wind chime rings its song today,
Upon my weary heart.

title: Old Stone Wall
lyrics:
An old stone wall through fields it winds,
Built by hands now gone,
The moss grows thick on each gray stone,
Where generations walked upon.

The wall may crumble, cracks appear,
Yet still it stands today,
An old stone wall a boundary,
That time could not decay.

title: Cemetery Walk
lyrics:
We walk through cemetery gates,
Where ancestors lie still,
The flags we place on Memorial Day,
Their service to fulfill.

The marble stones stand straight and true,
The names we barely read,
A cemetery walk reminds us,
Of those who came before.

title: Honeysuckle Vine
lyrics:
The honeysuckle vine climbs up,
The old wooden fence row,
Its fragrance sweet fills summer air,
Where bees and butterflies go.

The trumpet blooms of yellow white,
Invite the hummingbird,
A honeysuckle vine in bloom,
Is summer's sweet reward.

title: Picnic Blanket
lyrics:
The picnic blanket's spread out wide,
Beneath the oak tree shade,
The basket holds the lunch we packed,
Of sandwiches we made.

The ants parade in single file,
To our feast they come,
A picnic blanket on the grass,
Makes summer afternoons fun.

title: Old Accordion Song
lyrics:
The old accordion squeezes out,
A tune of polka beat,
Its buttons worn from years of play,
Make melodies complete.

The squeeze box plays for dancing feet,
Of those who still recall,
An old accordion playing songs,
That echo through the hall.

title: Morning Rooster
lyrics:
The rooster crows at break of day,
His voice rings far and wide,
He's proud to herald morning light,
With nature as his guide.

The chickens cluck and peck the ground,
The farm begins to wake,
A rooster crows to start the day,
A traditional song to make.

title: Campground Stars
lyrics:
The campground sits beneath the stars,
Where constellations bright,
The Big Dipper guides our way,
Through铺 from our campsite light.

The wood smoke rises to the sky,
The fire crackles low,
Campground stars above our heads,
Make darkness glow.

title: Apple Orchard
lyrics:
The apple orchard in the fall,
Is everyone's delight,
The leaves turn red and orange bright,
A painter's palette sight.

The cider fresh from pressing apples,
The donuts hot and sweet,
An apple orchard in autumn,
Is fall's delicious treat.

title: Wind Through Wheat
lyrics:
The wind blows through the wheat field,
Making waves like sea,
The amber stalks bend gracefully,
As far as eyes can see.

The harvest time is almost here,
The wheat so golden tall,
A wind through wheat is summer's gold,
That feeds both great and small.

title: Old Cash Register
lyrics:
The old cash register rings so loud,
With keys that jangle clear,
It counted pennies, nickels, dimes,
For all the goods stored here.

The drawer slides with mechanical click,
The totals all added right,
An old cash register ring,
Marked each transaction tight.

title: Telescope Gazing
lyrics:
I peer through the telescope,
At craters on the moon,
The Milky Way stretches across,
Like a river's tune.

The stars are twinkling far away,
Since billions of years ago,
Through telescope we see the past,
A cosmic light to show.

title: Honey Bee
lyrics:
The honey bee buzzes round the hive,
His stripes of yellow black,
He gathers nectar from each flower,
And builds his honeycomb stack.

The colony works all summer long,
To fill their golden store,
A honey bee is busy bee,
Who crafts the honey more.

title: Watering Can
lyrics:
I fill the watering can,
To help my garden grow,
Each flower stem and vegetable,
Needs water's gentle flow.

The sprinkler head sprays droplets fine,
Like summer rain so sweet,
A watering can does its job,
To make the garden complete.

title: Stepping Stones
lyrics:
The stepping stones across the pond,
Lead to the garden gate,
Each stone worn smooth by feet that walked,
And weather turned to slate.

I hop from stone to stone with care,
To avoid the lily pads,
Stepping stones across the pond,
Are paths my童年 once had.

title: Cottage Door
lyrics:
The cottage door is painted red,
With brass knocker worn,
Welcome mat says come on in,
And rest from wind that's blown.

Inside the fire crackles warm,
The tea pot whistles shrill,
A cottage door that welcomes all,
To find their peace and will.
